headline: GameStop Launches $55.5 Billion Bid to Acquire E-commerce Giant eBay
lede: The gaming retailer is attempting a radical transformation through a massive, debt-fueled takeover offer. Market analysts remain deeply skeptical of the operational synergy behind this high-stakes shift from brick-and-mortar storefronts to global e-commerce.

headline: Amsterdam Becomes First Global Capital to Ban Meat and Fossil Fuel Ads
lede: By treating high-carbon marketing similarly to tobacco, city officials are testing whether municipal advertising bans can effectively shift public social norms. The policy serves as a bold, controversial template for cities targeting 2050 carbon neutrality.
pullquote: Public space regulations are the new frontier for reshaping urban consumption habits.

headline: Project Maintainers Warn of Fraudulent Notepad++ Website Targeting Mac Users
lede: A malicious site is exploiting the Notepad++ trademark to distribute unauthorized, fake software to unsuspecting macOS users. The project creator has formally disavowed these claims and is actively pursuing legal recourse against the impersonators.

headline: Using Underdrawings to Fix Generative AI Accuracy Issues
lede: Generative models frequently struggle with spatial logic, particularly when rendering precise text and sequential numbers. This technique uses deterministic SVG templates to provide the structural scaffolding required for high-fidelity, accurate machine-generated output.

headline: Debunking the Myth of Long-Range Quantum Heartbeat Sensors
lede: Recent claims regarding CIA heartbeat detection via quantum magnetometry face insurmountable physical hurdles. Experts suggest the sensationalized narrative likely functions as a strategic intelligence cover for more conventional, classified tracking technologies.

headline: Teaching Computational Thinking Without Computers in NHK’s Texico
lede: Programming is often reduced to syntax, but this unique educational program focuses entirely on the logical frameworks of abstraction and simulation. By moving away from keyboards, the show invites learners to build foundational algorithmic skills through pure, animated problem-solving.

headline: BYOMesh Brings High-Bandwidth Dual-Radio Performance to LoRa Networking
lede: This compact developer kit bridges the gap between low-bandwidth radio networks and high-performance mesh backhaul. By integrating both sub-1GHz and 2.4GHz bands on a single board, the hardware aims to solve critical connectivity bottlenecks in long-range remote links.

headline: DeepClaude Proxy Cuts Agentic Coding Costs by Up to 90 Percent
lede: This utility intercepts Claude Code API calls to run autonomous agents on more cost-efficient models like DeepSeek. The architecture decouples the agentic interface from Anthropic's native backend, allowing developers to maintain familiar workflows at a fraction of the cost.

headline: Thermos Recalls Millions of Bottles After Reports of Severe Projectile Injuries
lede: A significant design defect in Thermos food jars has led to a massive safety recall following reports of permanent vision loss. The stoppers, lacking necessary pressure relief, are prone to forceful, dangerous ejection when opened.

headline: Reverse-Engineering Hard Disk Geometry Through External Microbenchmarking
lede: Modern storage interfaces abstract physical realities, yet latency-based timing reveals the complex mechanical dance of spinning platters. This methodology documents how to reconstruct internal hardware profiles in an era where physical geometry is hidden from the user.
